sprint: Sprint 34 -- V0.27.8 Bug Fixes (deploy-fix + sync data-hygiene; all Drive-12-independent)
spec: offices/pm/issues/I-031 (US-327 backfill breaks in deploy contexts) + offices/pm/backlog/B-078 (sync_history idle churn / SyncCadenceController not idling) + offices/pm/backlog/B-079 (sync_history started_at/completed_at TZ mismatch) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-051 (orphan-cleanup.service IO priority -- the durable complement to V0.27.7 US-330's race-guard)
backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint #7 in the V0.27 chain. Per CIO 2026-05-12 'is there anything that can be done independent of Drive 12? if yes build that sprint' -- 4 stories that need NO IRL drive/drain to validate (synthetic tests + deployed-Pi/server queries suffice). Per chain-end-merge rule: V0.27.8 branches FROM sprint/sprint33-bugfixes-V0.27.7 (cumulative chain), NOT from main. B-077 (connection_log chatter) deferred to V0.28 per the tester's lean (it's ~1.5 rows/min post-V0.27.7, down from the 6/min reading, partially covered by V0.27.6 US-325); the one-time connection_log + sync_history truncates ride B-076's cleanup step.

testBaseline.note: Post-V0.27.7 baseline (V0.27.6's 4161 + ~43 new tests across US-326/327/328/330). Verify exact count at sprint start. PRE-EXISTING ruff drift in tests/pi/regression/test_powersource_module_identity.py (8 UP037/F401 findings, V0.24.1 file) -- ruff-version drift since baseline; not in any sprint scope; flagged repeatedly since Sprint 32.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Close 4 bugs that don't need Drive 12 or Drain 18 to validate. (1) I-031: V0.27.7 US-327's deploy-server.sh Step 4.6 backfill mechanism fires but the backfill script itself fails in BOTH practical run-contexts -- run from the Windows dev box, Git-Bash MSYS path-mangles the Pi-side sqlite path (/home/... -> C:/Program Files/Git/home/...); run directly on chi-srv-01, loadServerCreds SSHes to 10.27.27.10 which IS chi-srv-01 -> Host key verification failed. Battery_health_log rows 11-15 stay NULL until this is fixed. (2) B-078: sync_history is at 20,381 rows (27 days) and growing ~1.5 rows/min even engine-off (15 rows in a 10-min sample) -- the Sprint 26 SyncCadenceController IDLE-60s isn't engaging; either it's not consulted by the live sync trigger, or it never releases from ACTIVE because the engine-off OBD poll loop keeps producing realtime_data rows. (3) B-079: every sync_history row has completed_at - started_at == exactly 18000s (5h CDT/UTC offset) -- one column written local-time, the other UTC -> the sync-duration metric is garbage. (4) TD-051: the V0.27.7 US-330 race-guard absorbs the journalctl-times-out-under-orphan-cleanup-IO symptom; the durable complement is throttling orphan-cleanup.service's I/O (IOSchedulingClass=idle) + ordering it After=eclipse-obd.service so the boot-time catch-up DELETE doesn't starve the launching orchestrator.
EXECUTION ORDER (parallel; no inter-dependencies): TIER 1 (P2) -- US-331 (I-031 deploy-fix) + US-332 (B-078 sync churn) + US-333 (B-079 TZ). TIER 2 (P3) -- US-334 (TD-051 orphan-cleanup IO).

THEME: 'Make the V0.27.7 deploy machinery actually work + stop the idle write storms.' V0.27.7 closed the Drive-11 analytics-tier gaps but left two operational residuals: the US-327 backfill that can't run in either real context (I-031), and the orphan-cleanup race that US-330 only band-aided (TD-051). Plus two standalone data-hygiene bugs the tester's 2026-05-12 db-review surfaced (B-078 sync churn, B-079 TZ). None of these need a drive or a drain to validate -- synthetic tests + a query against the already-deployed Pi/server are sufficient.

CONSTRAINTS (anti-blocker discipline + Sprint 28-33 lessons): (a) US-274 file-existence check ran during grooming. (b) US-282 commit-vs-claim verifier active. (c) feedback_pm_verify_diagnostic_premises.md: PM verified each premise empirically -- I-031 reproduced both failure contexts during the V0.27.7 deploy + a follow-up direct attempt; B-078 confirmed via sqlite3/mysql queries against the live Pi + server (20,381 rows, 15/10min idle); B-079 confirmed via the 18000s diff on every recent sync_history row. (d) feedback_pm_verify_table_names_against_code.md applied. (e) feedback_pm_run_pre_flight_during_grooming.md: PM ran the greps -- B-079's writer is in src/server/api/sync.py (also confirmed src/server/db/models.py:SyncHistory); B-078's SyncCadenceController was wired into core._maybeTriggerIntervalSync by Sprint 26 US-299 (Ralph re-confirms the exact module at pre-flight -- the network filesystem was slow during grooming so a full rg of src/pi/ timed out; this is disclosed, not skipped); I-031's root causes are in scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py + scripts/apply_server_migrations.py:loadServerCreds. (f) feedback_pm_main_merges_at_chain_end_only.md: V0.27.8 stays on the sprint branch; the V0.27 chain merges via /chain-validated once Drive 12 + Drain 18 are green. (g) feedback_pm_patch_version_bug_fix_sprint_pattern.md: V0.27.X = bug-fixes only; B-077 (connection_log chatter feature-ish 'slow it further') + B-076 (server schema epic) + B-074/B-075 (features) all stay V0.28+. (h) feedback_pm_validate_cli_in_cio_shell.md: US-331's fixes MUST be re-validated by running `bash deploy/deploy-server.sh` Step 4.6 from the CIO's Windows Git-Bash shell (the failing context), not just synthetic tests.

GROUNDING SOURCES: V0.27.7 sprint-deploy 2026-05-12 (the Step-4.6 failure output captured live: 'Error: unable to open database C:/Program Files/Git/home/...' from Windows; 'Host key verification failed' from chi-srv-01) + tester 2026-05-12 db-review note (offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-12-from-tester-db-review-validation-bug-vs-techdebt.md: sync_history 20,283 rows growing ~12/min + the 5h started_at/completed_at TZ mismatch; B-NEW-2 + B-NEW-3) + PM 2026-05-12 live re-query (sync_history 20,381 rows / 15 in 10 min idle; every row diff_s=18000) + V0.27.7 US-330 close note + TD-051 (the orphan-cleanup IO-priority filing).

POST-SPRINT-34 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO no-human-tasks rule): (1) CIO re-runs `bash deploy/deploy-server.sh` from Windows Git-Bash post-V0.27.8 -> Step 4.6 backfill completes; server battery_health_log rows 11-15 populated; rerun no-op (US-331 / I-031 real-world gate). (2) The V0.27 chain validation gate is unchanged: Drive 12 (full server pipeline -- drive_summary analytics + Approach-1 drive_statistics rows) + Drain 18 (clean startup_log prior_boot_clean=1). When BOTH green AND V0.27.8 is in the chain -> /chain-validated merges + cuts V0.28.0. (3) V0.28+ queue: B-074 (MAP PID) + B-075 (drive_statistics Approach 2) + B-076 (server schema normalization epic -- owns the deferred US-329 fix + DROP TABLE drive_counter + the one-time connection_log/sync_history truncates) + B-077 (connection_log chatter -- now low-urgency).

stories.title: US-327 backfill works in both deploy run-contexts -- I-031
stories.intent: I-031: V0.27.7 US-327 wired deploy-server.sh Step 4.6 (idempotent backfill of stranded server-side battery_health_log rows) + a --count-stranded pre-check. The plumbing fires (it found 5 stranded rows + triggered the backfill), but the backfill script itself fails in BOTH practical run-contexts: (Context 1, run from the Windows dev box via Git Bash) the Pi-side sqlite path `$HOME/Projects/Eclipse-01/data/obd.db` gets MSYS2 path-mangled to `C:/Program Files/Git/home/mcornelison/...` when interpolated into the remote `ssh ... sqlite3 -readonly <path>` command -> 'unable to open database file'. (Context 2, run directly on chi-srv-01) `loadServerCreds` (imported from apply_server_migrations.py) SSHes to mcornelison@10.27.27.10 to read DATABASE_URL -- but chi-srv-01 IS 10.27.27.10, so this ssh-to-self fails 'Host key verification failed'. The Step-4.6 wiring is correct; the host/path resolution under real conditions is the gap. Fix BOTH: (1) the remote-sqlite3 path is no longer MSYS-mangled from Git Bash (MSYS_NO_PATHCONV / single-quote so only the remote shell expands it / build the command so the leading `/` isn't treated as a Windows drive path); (2) when the server address resolves to localhost, loadServerCreds reads DATABASE_URL from the local .env instead of ssh-to-self (or accepts the self-loop host key gracefully).

filesToTouch: scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py (UPDATE -- the remote Pi-side sqlite3 invocation no longer MSYS-path-mangled from Git Bash; e.g. wrap with MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1 / single-quote the remote command so only the Pi's shell expands `$HOME/.../obd.db` / pass the path so the leading `/` isn't a Windows-drive trigger; mod-history US-331)

scripts/apply_server_migrations.py (UPDATE -- loadServerCreds: when the server address is localhost / resolves to this host, read DATABASE_URL from the local .env directly instead of `ssh mcornelison@<self>`; or fall back to `-o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new` for the self-loop; same fix benefits Step 4.5 migrations run on chi-srv-01)

tests/scripts/test_backfill_deploy_contexts.py (NEW -- regression test: (a) the remote-sqlite3 command string is not path-mangled when MSYS_NO_PATHCONV-style guarding is in effect / the path is single-quoted; (b) loadServerCreds with a localhost-ish server address reads .env locally and does NOT shell out to ssh; both would FAIL pre-fix)
filesToRead: scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py (the V0.27.7 US-327 script -- scanPiRows / _runServerSql / the remote-command construction; --count-stranded mode)
scripts/apply_server_migrations.py:loadServerCreds + loadAddresses (the shared ssh helpers; how the server address is resolved)
deploy/deploy-server.sh Step 4.5 + Step 4.6 (how the script is invoked LOCALLY; the --sentinel-dir ${TMPDIR:-/tmp} pattern)
doNotTouch: deploy/deploy-server.sh Step 4.6 wiring (the trigger logic is correct -- only the script's host/path resolution is broken; don't re-architect Step 4.6)
The --count-stranded mode behaviour / the idempotency guards (planBackfill skip-populated + `AND end_timestamp IS NULL` on every UPDATE) -- those are correct
Pi-side battery_health_log table or writer (rows 11-15 are correctly closed ON THE PI; the gap is server-side)

ERROR: reading Pi-side battery_health_log failed: Error: unable to open database C:/Program Files/Git/home/mcornelison/Projects/Eclipse-01/data/obd.db: unable to open database file. WARN: backfill did not complete (Pi unreachable?); rows stay stranded -- safe to retry next deploy.'

(chi-srv-01 IS 10.27.27.10 -- ssh-to-self host-key gap)

stories.title: sync_history idle churn -- SyncCadenceController IDLE engages -- B-078
stories.intent: B-078: sync_history is at 20,381 rows (since 2026-04-16, ~27 days) and growing ~1.5 rows/min even engine-off (15 rows in a 10-minute idle sample) -- one sync every ~40s. The Sprint 26 SyncCadenceController (US-298) was supposed to drop to IDLE-60s when there are no new local rows since the last successful sync; US-299 wired it into core._maybeTriggerIntervalSync. But it's not engaging IDLE -- either (a) it's not actually consulted by the live sync trigger (phantom-path drift), or (b) it never releases from ACTIVE because the engine-off OBD poll loop keeps producing realtime_data rows (so 'new local rows since last sync' is ~always true -> the controller never sees an idle window). 20k+ rows in 27 days, still climbing -- worth fixing before V0.28. Fix: confirm SyncCadenceController is consulted by the live trigger; make IDLE actually engage -- either IDLE = 'no drive in progress' regardless of routine engine-off poll rows, OR don't let routine engine-off poll rows reset the ACTIVE timer, OR (if it's the engine-off poll cadence that's the real driver) slow that down so there's a genuine idle window. Plus a one-time prune of historical sync_history rides B-076 (NOT this story).

filesToTouch: src/pi/sync/sync_cadence_controller.py (UPDATE -- the IDLE/ACTIVE state transition: routine engine-off poll rows must not pin it ACTIVE; e.g. ACTIVE requires a drive boundary / new rows other than the idle poll. Note: if pre-flight finds the live sync-interval trigger -- the consumer that Sprint 26 US-299 wired this into, likely in src/pi/obdii/orchestrator core or src/pi/sync/client.py -- ALSO needs a change to actually consult the controller, add that file at pre-flight per the scope-fence amendment process)

src/pi/sync/client.py (UPDATE if pre-flight confirms the sync trigger lives/passes through here -- ensure SyncCadenceController is consulted by the live interval trigger, not just instantiated; if the trigger is actually in the orchestrator core module instead, swap this entry for that one at pre-flight)

tests/pi/sync/test_sync_cadence_controller_idle.py (NEW -- 30-tick mocked run: engine-off (only routine poll rows since last sync) -> controller is IDLE-60s after the warm-up; drive in progress (drive_start + realtime_data) -> ACTIVE-5s; would FAIL pre-fix where it stays ACTIVE)
filesToRead: Sprint 26 US-298 + US-299 close notes (the SyncCadenceController state machine + the core._maybeTriggerIntervalSync wiring)
src/pi/ -- pre-flight: rg `SyncCadenceController|_maybeTriggerIntervalSync|tickIntervalSec|IDLE|ACTIVE` to find the state machine + its consumer + whether the consumer actually calls it
the engine-off OBD poll loop (what writes realtime_data when the engine is off -- its cadence is the likely driver of 'always new rows')
doNotTouch: ACTIVE-5s cadence during an actual drive (no regression on drive-time sync latency)

Tester 2026-05-12 db-review (B-NEW-2): 'sync runs ~every 5s when nothing new -- SyncCadenceController (B-053 IDLE-60s) not idling, or ACTIVE mode stuck on. 20k+ rows/few days unsustainable.'

Real-world validation gate: post-V0.27.8 deploy, leave the Pi engine-off for 20 min -> sync_history grows <= ~1 row/min (IDLE-60s engaged). During a drive (Drive 12+), ACTIVE-5s still engages (no regression). Both verified by a `SELECT COUNT(*) ... WHERE started_at > NOW() - INTERVAL N MINUTE` query.

stories.title: sync_history started_at + completed_at both written in UTC -- B-079
stories.intent: B-079: every recent sync_history row has `completed_at - started_at == exactly 18000s` (5h00m00s = America/Chicago CDT offset) -- one of the two columns is written with a local-time clock, the other with UTC, within the same row. The sync-duration metric (`completed_at - started_at`) is therefore garbage (always ~5h). TD-027-class bug: a naive-timestamp writer that escaped the Sprint 14 sweep. The writer lives in src/server/api/sync.py (confirmed via grep; src/server/db/models.py:SyncHistory is the model). Fix: make both columns use UTC per the TD-027 / specs/standards.md convention (datetime.now(timezone.utc) on the Python side / canonical ISO-8601 UTC) -- whichever of started_at/completed_at is currently wrong gets flipped to match the correct one.

filesToTouch: src/server/api/sync.py (UPDATE -- the sync_history row writer: both `started_at` and `completed_at` written with the same UTC-aware clock; mod-history US-333 + TD-027 cross-ref)

tests/server/test_sync_history_timestamps_utc.py (NEW -- regression test: a synthetic sync writes a sync_history row; assert `completed_at >= started_at` AND `completed_at - started_at < 60s` (a real sync duration, not ~18000s); would FAIL pre-fix where the diff is exactly 5h)
filesToRead: src/server/api/sync.py (the sync endpoint -- where sync_history rows are written; find which of started_at/completed_at uses the wrong clock)
src/server/db/models.py:SyncHistory (the model -- column defaults; if a column has a server_default of local NOW(), that's the bug)
specs/standards.md timestamp convention + the TD-027 sweep (Sprint 14 US-202/US-203) for the canonical UTC pattern
doNotTouch: Other timestamp columns / other writers (scope is sync_history.started_at + completed_at only -- a wider TD-027 re-sweep is out of scope)
sync_history schema beyond the two columns' write path (no migration needed -- the columns exist; only the values are wrong)
Historical rows -- a one-time fixup is low value (the B-076 prune likely truncates most of them); not in scope

groundingRefs.value: PM 2026-05-12 live query: `SELECT id, started_at, completed_at, TIMESTAMPDIFF(SECOND, started_at, completed_at) diff_s FROM sync_history ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 4` -> every row diff_s == 18000 (e.g. id=20381: started_at 2026-05-12 20:41:36, completed_at 2026-05-13 01:41:36)
Tester 2026-05-12 db-review (B-NEW-3): 'sync_history.started_at and completed_at written in different timezones (5h CDT/UTC offset) in the same row. Pick one (UTC) for both.'
Real-world validation gate: post-V0.27.8 deploy -> new sync_history rows have `completed_at - started_at` in the seconds range (a real sync duration), not ~18000s; both columns reflect UTC wall-clock

acceptance: Pre-flight: locate the sync_history writer in src/server/api/sync.py; identify which of started_at/completed_at uses the wrong (local) clock vs UTC -- and whether it's a Python-side datetime.now() (no tz) or a model server_default of local NOW()
Both columns written with the same UTC-aware clock; new rows have `completed_at - started_at` in the seconds range
Regression test tests/server/test_sync_history_timestamps_utc.py FAILS pre-fix (diff ~18000s), PASSES post-fix (diff < 60s)
Fast suite GREEN; no other sync_history-touching test regresses
invariants: Real-world gate: new rows show a seconds-range duration
Scope fence: only sync_history.started_at + completed_at -- not a wider TD-027 re-sweep
stopConditions: If pre-flight reveals BOTH columns are actually UTC and the 5h is an artifact of the query session's TZ (not the stored values) -- STOP, document; the bug would then be a reporting/display issue, not a writer bug -- PM re-scopes
If fixing the wrong column requires a model migration (e.g. changing a server_default) rather than a write-path change -- STOP, document; PM approves the migration vs a Python-side override
doNotTouch: Other timestamp columns / writers (no wider TD-027 re-sweep)

stories.title: orphan-cleanup.service I/O throttle + ordering -- TD-051
stories.intent: TD-051: V0.27.7 US-330 fixed the I-030 startup_log regression with a race-guard (detectBootReason retries `journalctl --list-boots` 3x when it times out under boot-time I/O contention). The root *cause* of that contention is the V0.27.6 US-322 orphan-cleanup.service: its timer has Persistent=true (so it fires at boot to catch up the missed nightly 03:00 run) and the service runs `cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py --execute` -- a DELETE against the Pi 5 SD-card SQLite DB -- concurrently with the launching eclipse-obd orchestrator. The race-guard absorbs the symptom; the durable complement is to (a) throttle orphan-cleanup.service's I/O via `IOSchedulingClass=idle` (best-effort -> only runs when nothing else needs the disk) and (b) order it `After=eclipse-obd.service` so the boot-time catch-up DELETE doesn't starve the orchestrator's early `journalctl` calls. Low urgency (the race-guard is sufficient); this is hardening + a cleaner boot.

filesToTouch: deploy/orphan-cleanup.service (UPDATE -- add `IOSchedulingClass=idle` (and consider `Nice=19` / `IOSchedulingPriority=7`) to [Service]; add `After=eclipse-obd.service` to [Unit]; mod-history US-334. deploy-pi.sh's step_install_orphan_cleanup_unit already syncs this file if-changed -- no deploy-pi.sh change needed unless pre-flight finds the unit content is also templated there)

tests/deploy/test_orphan_cleanup_install.py (NEW -- assert the installed/synced orphan-cleanup.service contains `IOSchedulingClass=idle` + `After=eclipse-obd.service`; mirror the pattern in tests/deploy/test_drain_forensics_install.py / test_journald_persistent_install.py)

doNotTouch: src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py (the US-330 race-guard stays -- this story is the *complement*, not a replacement; belt-and-suspenders)
cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py business logic (only the systemd unit's resource/ordering directives change, not what the cleanup does)
orphan-cleanup.timer schedule (the nightly 03:00 OnCalendar + Persistent=true stay -- we're throttling the run, not changing when it's scheduled)

groundingRefs.value: V0.27.7 US-330 close note: the I-030 both-NULL startup_log row traces to `journalctl --list-boots` returning None (TimeoutExpired -- the 10s JOURNALCTL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS cap blown under boot-time SD-card I/O contention); the V0.27.5->V0.27.6 delta that fits the regression cliff is US-322's orphan-cleanup.timer (Persistent=true -> fires at boot, runs a DELETE on data/obd.db concurrently with the launching orchestrator). Filed as TD-051; the race-guard (3x list-boots retry) absorbs the symptom.

Pi deploy 2026-05-12 output confirms the orphan-cleanup unit + timer are installed + enabled: 'Installing orphan-cleanup systemd unit (US-322 / B-072, sync-if-changed) -- orphan-cleanup.service already up-to-date. orphan-cleanup.timer enabled + active.'

Real-world validation gate: post-V0.27.8 deploy + a Pi reboot (e.g. after Drain 18) -> the orphan-cleanup boot-time catch-up run does not delay the orchestrator's startup_log write (prior_boot_clean populated -- same as US-330's gate, just with the contention removed at the source rather than retried around); `systemctl show orphan-cleanup.service -p IOSchedulingClass -p After` reflects the new settings

ruff check (any touched Python -- likely none beyond a test helper)
invariants: deploy-pi.sh stays idempotent (sync-if-changed; re-deploy is a no-op once the unit matches)
stopConditions: If the orphan-cleanup unit is defined as a heredoc inside deploy-pi.sh rather than a standalone deploy/orphan-cleanup.service file -- that's fine, edit the heredoc; but if editing it would require restructuring step_install_orphan_cleanup_unit -- STOP, document; PM evaluates extracting the unit to a file vs an in-place heredoc edit

If `After=eclipse-obd.service` creates an ordering cycle with anything orphan-cleanup.timer depends on -- STOP, document; fall back to IOSchedulingClass=idle alone (the I/O throttle is the load-bearing part; the ordering is the cherry)
doNotTouch: src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py (the US-330 race-guard)
cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py business logic
orphan-cleanup.timer schedule

bigDefinitionOfDone: `bash deploy/deploy-server.sh` from the CIO's Windows Git-Bash shell -> Step 4.6 completes; server-side battery_health_log rows 11-15 populated with end_timestamp/end_soc/runtime_seconds matching Pi-side; a 2nd deploy is a no-op (US-331 / I-031)
Pi engine-off for 20 min post-V0.27.8 deploy -> sync_history grows <= ~1 row/min (IDLE-60s engaged); ACTIVE-5s still engages during Drive 12+ (US-332 / B-078)
New sync_history rows have `completed_at - started_at` in the seconds range (a real sync duration), not ~18000s; both columns UTC (US-333 / B-079)

orphan-cleanup.service shows IOSchedulingClass=idle + After=eclipse-obd.service post-deploy; a Pi reboot's boot-time catch-up cleanup doesn't delay the orchestrator's startup_log write (US-334 / TD-051)
